title: St. David's, Pembroke & Solva (Pembrokeshire)

main_route.excerpt: We reached Hundred House**** west of Kington west of Leominster at 9.10. Cold wind.

We drove on to Pembroke Castle. It has the same layout as Sudley + Ludlow. The most amazing part of it is Wogan's Cavern, where supplies from boats were unloaded + carried up a spiral staircase into the castle.
We drove on to Solva, but rainsqualls were so heavy that we didn't get out of the van.

main_route.normalized_name: Rhosson Garn Campsite, near St. David's, Pembrokeshire, Wales
main_route.excerpt: We reached the campsite. Rhosson Gand ***** as good a crew as the Normandy campsite, but no lighthouse. Absolutely fabulous seascape. Heavy rain crashing waves.
Arrived at Cleobury Mortimer + had fish + chips.
Then we called in at Trumpley to see Jim + Margaret + Helen, on our way home.
Then we called in at Trumpley to see Jim + Margaret + Helen, on our way home.

excursions.label: Cycle to St. David's Cathedral (Sunday 26 May)

stops.normalized_name: St. David's Cathedral, St. David's, Pembrokeshire, Wales
stops.excerpt: We cycled off to the cathedral for an 11.15 Eucharist.

excursions.label: Walk to Solva and coastal path (Monday 27 May)

stops.excerpt: We drove to Solva + parked on the quay. There were young people learning to canoe. The village is pretty but a bit too pretty.

excursions.label: St. David's Cathedral, craft fair, and Llandygwydd etching artist (Tuesday 28 May)

stops.normalized_name: St. David's Cathedral, St. David's, Pembrokeshire, Wales
stops.excerpt: We drove into St. Davids + looked at the Cathedral, Holy Trinity Chapel + miserichords again. Then we saw a craft fair.
we set off for his workshop the other side of Fishguard to the north. He framed the etching in a lighter colour wood while we had lunch in the van in his village, Llandygwydd.

unresolved.issue: A campsite associated with a person named Joan, surname uncertain (transcribed as [?Tillett]); located near the coastal path past the golf course from Rhosson Garn. Not identifiable as a named place.
unresolved.excerpt: We walked past the golf course to see Joan [?Tillett]'s campsite.

boundary_flags.note: Page A_0310_R begins mid-page; the preceding text describes a different outing (pub lunch at Hadnall, Prees). The trip narrative begins partway through the page with 'Friday May 24th'.
Page A_0313_R has an erroneous transcription date of 1996-05-28; context confirms this is 2002-05-28 (Tuesday May 28th fits 2002 calendar, and the diary year is 2002 throughout).

The diary refers to the dog as 'Tonic' ('Simon, Eve & Tonic left for St. David's'). Family context names the dog as 'Tinder'. This may be a transcription variant or a different dog name used informally.

summary: Simon and Eve (with their dog) spent four nights at the five-star Rhosson Garn campsite near St. David's in Pembrokeshire, 24-28 May 2002. En route they stopped at Pembroke Castle (including Wogan's Cavern) and drove through Solva; from the base they made day trips to St. David's Cathedral, Solva harbour, and a local etching artist's studio in Llandygwydd near Fishguard, returning home via the Elan Valley, Cleobury Mortimer, and a visit to friends at Trumpley.
extraction_notes: 1. Travellers: only Simon and Eve are named; no children are mentioned (Peter is noted as being in Tibet for his birthday). The dog, called 'Tonic' in the diary, accompanies them. 2. Rhosson Gand (diary spelling) is Rhosson Garn, a headland/farm near St. David's — the campsite is in this area. 3. Pembroke Castle and the first Solva drive-through were on Saturday 25 May (en route to campsite), not excursions. 4. The return routing via Tregaron and Elan Valley is clearly narrated but these are mentioned as passing-through points only ('driving to Beulah (24 mi)', 'stopped for tea s. of Elan valley') — Tregaron and Elan Valley south are transit references without a stop explicit enough to add; Beulah is also transited. 5. Llandygwydd is north of Fishguard (Ceredigion), as the diary says 'the other side of Fishguard to the north'. 6. Fishguard is mentioned only as a geographic reference for Llandygwydd's location, not as a stop visited.
